title: Error message 'All elements of Enum data type must be of form ...' is not quite accurate
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
This is a very very minor issue.
```
:) create table tttm2 (f Enum('A', 'AA', 'B'=42, 'C'=43)) Engine=MergeTree order by tuple();
```
fails with
```
Received exception:
Code: 223. DB::Exception: All elements of Enum data type must be of form: 'name' = number or 'name', where name is string literal and number is an integer. (UNEXPECTED_AST_STRUCTURE)
```
Although
```
:) create table tttm1 (f Enum('A', 'B'=42, 'C'=43)) Engine=MergeTree order by tuple();
```
works Ok, although the constraint  "All elements of Enum data type must be of form" is violated (if we read it as "only one form is allowed")
Options
1. Do nothing
2. Do nothing but change error message to something else (I've tried, but seems unreadable)
3. Properly prohibit this mix and change message to something like "Enum elements must be either all explicitly assigned integer values ('name' = number) or all implicitly assigned ('name'). Mixing both styles in the same Enum is not allowed"
4. Allow this mix in a more smart and flexible way, e.g. to make Enum('A', 'AA', 'B'=42, 'C'=43, 'Z') is equivalent to Enum('A'=0, 'AA'=1, 'B'=42, 'C'=43, 'Z'=43)
I suppose that (3) is Ok and current behavior is not intentional.
I can implement if it is confirmed.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.12.2.54
### How to reproduce
See description
